title: astragalus centroalpinus
intro: Astragalus centroalpinus, also known as Central Asian milkvetch, is a perennial legume native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Astragalus centroalpinus are said to include reducing fatigue, improving immune function, and preventing age-related diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. Astragalus centroalpinus is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and can also be consumed as a raw herb or added to soups and other culinary dishes.
